channel.title: Paper Feeds (arXiv)
channel.description: Keyword-based research paper feeds from arXiv

item.title: IntraShuffler: A Privacy Preserving Framework for Heterogeneous DP Federated Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02563v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

在异构差分隐私联邦学习（HDP-FL）中，各客户端可依据数据敏感性与机构策略自主设定隐私预算 $\varepsilon_i$，服务器通过 $\varepsilon$-感知聚合（如按 $\varepsilon_i$ 加权）提升模型效用。然而，非独立同分布（non-IID）数据导致梯度更新携带显著结构性模式；当服务器结合隐私预算信息进行加权时，这些隐含信号会加剧隐私泄露风险——即使服务器是“诚实但好奇”的，也能利用梯度去噪与代理建模发起隐私推理攻击（Privacy Inference Attack），准确推断客户端数据分布属性（如类别偏置、设备类型），并跨轮次关联同一客户端的更新（链接成功率显著高于基线）。

方法创新：IntraShuffler 框架

为兼顾 HDP-FL 的效用优势与抗推理能力，本文提出 IntraShuffler——一种轻量级中间件防御框架。其核心是隐私感知的桶内混洗机制：

  • 首先将客户端按 $\varepsilon_i$ 相容性（如 $\varepsilon_i \in [a,b]$）聚类为“隐私兼容桶”，确保桶内梯度可安全加权；
  • 进而在每个桶内对模型参数（而非完整模型）执行细粒度、随机化 shuffling，打破梯度结构的时空一致性；
  • 关键设计：shuffling 在参数维度进行，不破坏 $\varepsilon$-感知聚合的数学基础，允许服务器仍按声明预算加权，保障模型收敛性与效用。

实验结果

在 CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100、FEMNIST 和 Shakespeare 四个 benchmark 数据集上验证：

  • 梯度可恢复性降低 >60%（基于重构误差与相似度指标）；
  • 代理推理准确率从 0.78 降至 0.33，接近随机猜测水平；
  • 模型精度损失 <1.2%（vs. 原始 HDP-FL），在 FedAvg、FedProx、SCAFFOLD 等多种聚合规则下均保持鲁棒性。

IntraShuffler 是首个在不牺牲 HDP-FL 核心机制前提下实现强推理防御的实用方案。

AI Summary (English)

Heterogeneous Differential Privacy Federated Learning (HDP-FL) enables clients to set individual privacy budgets ($\varepsilon_i$), and servers improve utility via $\varepsilon$-aware aggregation—yet this exposes structural gradient patterns from non-IID data, enabling honest-but-curious servers to launch Privacy Inference Attacks: inferring client data distributions and linking updates across rounds. While the Shuffle Model anonymizes sources, it is fundamentally incompatible with $\varepsilon$-aware aggregation. To bridge this gap, we propose IntraShuffler, a middleware framework that groups clients into privacy-compatible buckets based on $\varepsilon_i$ ranges and performs parameter-level shuffling within each bucket. This disrupts persistent gradient structure without breaking $\varepsilon$-weighted aggregation. Experiments on four datasets show IntraShuffler reduces gradient recoverability by >60%, drops surrogate inference accuracy from 0.78 to 0.33, and preserves model utility—achieving <1.2% accuracy loss under FedAvg, FedProx, and SCAFFOLD. It is the first practical defense enabling strong inference resistance while fully retaining HDP-FL’s utility-preserving aggregation.

Abstract

Heterogeneous Differential Privacy (HDP) in Federated Learning (FL) allows clients to select individual privacy budgets ($\varepsilon_i$) according to institutional policies and data sensitivity. In practice, many HDP-FL systems employ $\varepsilon$-aware server aggregation to improve model utility by re-weighting client updates according to their declared privacy budgets. However, gradient updates in FL retain structural patterns induced by non-independent and identically-distributed (non-IID) data, and these additional signals exposed by $\varepsilon$-aware aggregation create new opportunities for inference by an honest-but-curious server. In this work, we first show that a server equipped with gradient denoising and surrogate modeling can mount a \emph{Privacy Inference Attack} that infers distributional attributes of clients and links updates from the same client across training rounds, measured via surrogate inference accuracy and linkage success, under realistic knowledge constraints. The Shuffle-Model has been widely studied as a defense against such inference risks by anonymizing update sources, but it is fundamentally incompatible with HDP-FL $\varepsilon$-aware aggregation. To address this challenge, we propose \textbf{IntraShuffler}, a middleware defense framework designed for HDP-FL systems. IntraShuffler introduces a privacy-aware shuffling mechanism that groups clients into privacy-compatible buckets and performs parameter-level shuffling within each bucket to disrupt persistent gradient structure while preserving $\varepsilon$-aware aggregation. Experiments across four different datasets show that IntraShuffler reduces gradient recoverability by over 60% and decreases surrogate inference accuracy from 0.78 to 0.33 while maintaining comparable model utility across multiple FL aggregation rules.

item.title: Auditing Asset-Specific Preferences in Financial Large Language Models: Evidence from Bitcoin Representations and Portfolio Allocation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02528v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

大型语言模型（LLMs）正日益驱动智能投顾与交易代理，但其是否内嵌对特定金融资产的系统性偏好，迄今缺乏实证审计。本文聚焦比特币（Bitcoin），首次构建资产特异性偏好审计框架，系统探究三个核心问题：（1）LLMs是否对特定金融工具存在稳定排名偏差；（2）能否识别对其偏好具有因果影响力的关键内部表征；（3）该表征是否实质性影响下游投资决策（如资产配置）。

方法：三层次可解释性审计协议

1. 行为层审计：对8个前沿LLM（如Gemma 3、Llama 3、Claude 3等）开展框架敏感性测试。结果发现，比特币排名高度依赖语境——在“可靠货币”框架下平均排第5/8，而在“危机货币”或“自主代理”框架下跃升至前2位；属性交换实验进一步证实，模型响应由功能属性（如去中心化、抗审查）驱动，而非名称本身。
2. 机制层审计：在Gemma 3中扫描数千个稀疏自编码器（SAE）特征，定位到一个强选择性比特币表征特征（Feature ID: gemma3-4b-sae-16384-00027893）。通过激活/抑制该特征（prompt中不出现“Bitcoin”一词），可双向调控模型对比特币的偏好强度。
3. 决策层审计：在模拟资产配置任务中，特征放大使比特币组合占比提升5.2个百分点，抑制则降低4.6个百分点；且效应具有结构性：放大仅重配加密资产内部权重，抑制则显著削减整体加密敞口（-7.3 pp）。

创新贡献

提出有界行为杠杆（Bounded Behavioral Leverage）概念：单一内部特征可被精准干预以改变金融输出，但效应受限于模型架构与任务边界。本框架首次实现从神经元活动→偏好表达→真实投资决策的因果链验证，为新兴“知彼代理”（Know-Your-Agent, KYA）监管标准提供可测量的行为层基础。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces the first audit framework for asset-specific preferences in financial LLMs, using Bitcoin as a test case. We conduct a three-level analysis: (1) Behavioral audit across 8 frontier models reveals frame-dependent Bitcoin rankings—mid-tier as “reliable money” but top-tier under crisis/autonomy frames, with attribute-swaps confirming functional (not lexical) drivers; (2) Mechanistic audit identifies a dominant Bitcoin-selective sparse autoencoder feature in Gemma 3: amplifying it increases Bitcoin preference even without the word “Bitcoin” in prompts, while suppression reduces it; (3) Decision audit shows this feature causally shifts portfolio allocation—amplification raises Bitcoin’s share by +5.2 percentage points (reallocating within crypto), suppression lowers it by −4.6 pp (cutting total crypto exposure by −7.3 pp). We formalize this as bounded behavioral leverage: an internal feature exerts causal, measurable, and constrained influence over financial outputs. Validated via random controls and boundary tests, our work establishes a foundational behavioral layer for emerging Know-Your-Agent (KYA) standards.

Abstract

Large language models now power robo-advisors and trading agents, yet whether they carry built-in biases toward specific assets is largely untested. We ask three questions: do LLMs systematically prefer certain financial instruments; can an internal representation with causal leverage over those preferences be identified; and does that representation affect downstream financial decisions? We develop a three-level audit protocol and apply it to Bitcoin. First, a behavioral audit of eight frontier LLMs shows that Bitcoin's ranking among money-like instruments is frame-dependent: models place it around rank 5 of 8 as "reliable money" but near the top under crisis and autonomous-agent frames, and an attribute-swap experiment confirms rankings track functional properties, not names. Second, we open a model's internals: a search across thousands of sparse-autoencoder features in Gemma 3 identifies a dominant Bitcoin-selective feature. Amplifying it shifts the model toward the asset and suppressing it shifts the model away, even when "Bitcoin" never appears in the prompt. Third, we test financial consequences: amplification raises Bitcoin's portfolio share by 5.2 percentage points while suppression lowers it by 4.6 pp, with amplification reallocating within crypto and suppression cutting total crypto exposure. We characterize this as bounded behavioral leverage (leverage meaning causal influence over outputs, not financial leverage): an identifiable internal feature can be perturbed to move financial choices, but only within measurable limits. The framework links internal representations to external recommendations, validated with random controls and mechanism boundaries. As LLMs become autonomous financial agents, this is a first step toward a behavioral layer for emerging know-your-agent (KYA) standards: knowing what an agent prefers, and how far that preference can be moved.

item.title: Cross-modal linkage risk in clinical vision-language models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02276v1

AI Summary (中文)

跨模态链接风险：临床视觉-语言模型中的隐私新挑战

本研究首次系统揭示了临床视觉-语言模型（VLMs）中固有的跨模态重链接风险：当模型在配对的胸部X光片与放射科报告上训练后，其共享嵌入空间会隐式编码图像与文本之间的实例级对应关系。这一特性在医疗数据治理中构成显著隐私威胁——即使影像与报告在部署后被物理隔离（如仅共享脱敏影像、报告受严格访问控制），攻击者仍可仅凭余弦相似度，将一张匿名X光片重新关联至其原始结构化报告，无需额外元数据或模型逆向工程。

我们构建了严格的图像→报告检索审计框架，利用MIMIC-CXR（43,793个预留配对）与外部CheXpert Plus（29,296对）共406,241个真实配对样本（覆盖126,804名患者）作为基准，而非隐私场景本身。结果表明：模型临床专业化程度越高，重链接能力越强。最强VLM在候选池N=100时准确召回率达随机基线的15倍；N=10,000时达50倍；全库规模下仍显著高于偶然水平。该信号在引入病理匹配的难负样本（排除疾病标签捷径）后依然稳健，证实其捕获的是细粒度临床语义对齐，而非粗略诊断类别映射。

为规避昂贵重训练，我们提出靶向差分隐私微调：冻结图像/文本编码器，仅对定义跨模态对齐的投影头施加DP优化（ε=0.34, δ=6×10⁻⁶）。该方法在MIMIC-CXR上使N=10,000时Recall@1下降61.8%，且零样本迁移至CheXpert Plus；同时影像端临床效用几乎无损：14类疾病线性探针宏AUROC仅从79.63%微降至79.43%。本工作为平衡多模态AI的临床价值与患者隐私提供了首个可验证、可部署的隐私增强范式。

AI Summary (English)

This study identifies and quantifies a novel privacy risk—cross-modal linkage—in clinical vision-language models (VLMs): trained on paired chest radiographs and reports, VLMs implicitly encode instance-level image-report correspondence in their shared embedding space. We audit this via image-to-report retrieval on 406,241 ground-truth pairs from MIMIC-CXR and CheXpert Plus, finding re-linkage probability rises systematically with clinical specialization (e.g., 50× chance level at N=10,000 candidates). The signal persists under pathology-matched hard negatives, confirming fine-grained semantic alignment beyond diagnostic labels. To mitigate risk without retraining, we apply differential privacy only to the projection heads (ε=0.34, δ=6×10⁻⁶), freezing encoders. This reduces Recall@1 by 61.8% at N=10,000 on MIMIC-CXR and transfers zero-shot to CheXpert Plus, while preserving image utility: macro AUROC for 14-label classification drops only from 79.63% to 79.43%. Our targeted DP finetuning offers a practical, privacy-preserving path for clinical VLM deployment.

Abstract

Vision-language models (VLMs) trained on paired chest radiographs and radiology reports learn a shared embedding space that can preserve instance-level image-report correspondence. This poses a privacy risk in settings where radiographs and reports are deliberately kept separate after acquisition, such as image-only data sharing or access-controlled reports, because a de-identified image may be re-linked to its original narrative report through cosine similarity alone. We formalized this as image-to-report retrieval and used public paired cohorts, in which the true pairing is known by design, as ground-truth benchmarks to audit the risk rather than as the privacy scenario. Evaluating VLMs of increasing clinical specialization on 406,241 paired examples from 126,804 patients across MIMIC-CXR (43,793 held-out pairs) and external CheXpert Plus (29,296 pairs), we found that re-linkage rose systematically with specialization: the strongest VLM retrieved the correct report at 15 times chance at a candidate pool of N = 100, 50 times chance at N = 10,000, and well above chance at full-database scale. The signal persisted under pathology-matched hard negatives that removed disease-label shortcuts, indicating correspondence beyond broad diagnostic categories. To reduce it without retraining, we froze both encoders and applied differentially private optimization only to the projection heads defining the alignment layer (epsilon = 0.34, delta = 6x10-6). This reduced Recall@1 by 61.8% at N = 10,000 on MIMIC-CXR and transferred to CheXpert Plus without retraining, while image-side utility was largely preserved: macro AUROC for linear-probe classification across 14 labels shifted only from 79.63% to 79.43%. Targeted DP finetuning of the shared alignment layer can substantially reduce cross-modal re-linkage without materially degrading the image representations that make these models clinically useful.

item.title: ArrythML: An Autoencoder-Based TinyML Approach for On-Device Arrhythmia Detection on Resource-Constrained Embedded Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02256v1

AI Summary (中文)

ArrythML：面向资源受限嵌入式设备的自编码器驱动TinyML心律失常检测方法

本研究提出ArrythML——一种专为低功耗嵌入式可穿戴设备设计的端侧心律失常实时检测框架。针对传统云端依赖型ECG分析存在的隐私泄露、网络延迟与能耗高等问题，我们构建了轻量级、INT8量化、自编码器（Autoencoder）驱动的TinyML模型，仅含极简层数与参数（<100K可训练参数），支持在内存与算力严重受限的微控制器上高效部署。模型基于MIT-BIH心律失常数据库构建定制化标注数据集（含95,000+ ECG片段），涵盖室性早搏、室上性心动过速等12类临床相关心律异常。实验在PC仿真与真实硬件双轨验证：所有推理均在ESP32-S3微控制器（搭载TensorFlow Lite Micro运行时）上完成，实现端到端纯本地处理。创新性地开展细粒度失效归因分析——包括逐标注点（annotation-wise）与逐记录（record-wise）诊断，揭示模型对早期/隐匿性异常模式（如微小T波异位、PR间期渐进性延长）的高度敏感性；部分“误检”实为参考标注未覆盖的临床前征兆，印证模型潜在的预警价值。经剔除标注模糊样本后的严谨评估，最优DNN自编码器模型达成：84%召回率、79% F1分数、180 KB模型体积、单次推理仅9 ms延迟。该成果首次系统验证了超低功耗（<10 mW）、零数据上传、全栈嵌入式心律失常筛查的可行性，为隐私优先的下一代数字健康监护设备提供了可落地的技术范式。

AI Summary (English)

ArrythML introduces an autoencoder-based TinyML framework for real-time, on-device arrhythmia detection on ultra-resource-constrained embedded systems (e.g., ESP32-S3). We design INT8-quantized, minimal-parameter autoencoders (<100K params) optimized for TensorFlow Lite Micro, trained and evaluated on a curated subset of MIT-BIH Arrhythmia Database (95,000+ ECG segments). Rigorous validation includes PC simulation and in situ microcontroller deployment, complemented by annotation-wise and record-wise failure analysis to interpret model behavior across diverse morphologies. Notably, some “false positives” reflect clinically subtle or pre-symptomatic anomalies missed in ground-truth labels—highlighting the model’s sensitivity. After filtering ambiguous annotations, the best DNN autoencoder achieves 84% recall, 79% F1-score, ~180 KB size, and 9 ms on-device latency, demonstrating feasibility of privacy-preserving, battery-efficient wearable arrhythmia screening with full inference at the edge.

Abstract

Our work presents a method for ECG segmentation and arrhythmia detection using Tiny Machine Learning (TinyML) models for real-time, on-device inference on resource-constrained embedded systems. We develop INT8 quantized autoencoder-based TinyML models with minimal layers and parameters for embedded deployment. These models are evaluated using a custom dataset derived from the MIT-BIH Arrhythmia Database and validated in both PC-based simulations and on-device environments. For the evaluations, over 95,000 ECG segments are processed on an ESP32-S3 microcontroller running the TensorFlow Lite Micro runtime. Post-evaluation, detailed analysis, including annotation-wise and record-wise failure analysis, is conducted to characterize model behavior across diverse ECG morphologies and rhythm patterns and to explain missed detections. In several cases, apparent misclassifications may correspond to early or subtle anomaly patterns labeled as normal in the reference annotations, highlighting the model's sensitivity. A refined evaluation by filtering out ambiguous cases in the dataset shows that the best-performing DNN-based autoencoder achieves a recall of 84%, an F1-score of 79%, a model size of approximately 180 KB, and an inference latency of 9 ms on-device. These results demonstrate the feasibility of low-power, privacy-preserving embedded wearable systems capable of performing accurate arrhythmia detection entirely on-device.

item.title: Closing the Alignment-Maturity Gap in Federated Prototype Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02172v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在联邦学习（FL）中，从分布式、异构数据中学习判别性视觉表征面临根本性困难。基于原型（prototype-based）的方法虽能缓解统计异质性，但引入了距离依赖的对齐压力：早期全局原型尚不成熟（由噪声较大的本地原型聚合而成），强行对其对齐会生成过大梯度，抑制本地判别结构的形成，导致嵌入空间混乱、识别性能显著下降——该问题在强非独立同分布（non-IID）条件下尤为突出。

方法创新：FedSAP框架

我们提出FedSAP（Federated Scheduled Alignment and Proxy separation），通过两大协同机制闭环解决“对齐-成熟度失配”问题：

  • 确定性对齐课程（Deterministic Alignment Curriculum）：动态延迟全局对齐时机，仅当本地表征趋于稳定后才启动原型同步，避免早期内耗；
  • 几何驱动的代理分离损失（Geometry-driven Proxy Separation Loss）：在单位超球面上直接优化类间结构，复用现有原型库，零新增参数、零通信开销，实现紧凑且高可分的类簇组织。

主要成果

在三个标准基准（CIFAR-10/100, Tiny-ImageNet）及多级异质性设置下，FedSAP相较主流原型基线（如FedProto、FedPA）平均提升2.3–4.0个百分点，且异质性越强，增益越显著（+4.0% @ Dir(0.1)）。其表征本质天然支持半监督扩展：仅需微调标签掩码即可融合无标注数据，验证了“调度式对齐”作为通用设计范式的普适性与可扩展性。

AI Summary (English)

Learning discriminative representations in federated learning (FL) is hindered by a critical alignment-maturity gap: immature global prototypes—aggregated from noisy local ones—induce excessive gradient pressure during early rounds, degrading embedding structure and accuracy under severe non-IID data. We propose FedSAP, a lightweight yet effective framework featuring: (i) a deterministic alignment curriculum that postpones global prototype synchronization until local representations stabilize; and (ii) a geometry-driven proxy separation loss that enforces inter-class separation on the unit hypersphere using only the existing prototype bank—introducing no extra parameters or communication overhead. Evaluated across three benchmarks (CIFAR-10/100, Tiny-ImageNet) and diverse heterogeneity levels, FedSAP consistently outperforms prototype-based baselines by up to +4.0 percentage points, with largest gains under high non-IIDness (e.g., Dir(0.1)). Its representation-centric design further enables seamless semi-supervised extension with minimal modification, establishing scheduled alignment as a generalizable FL principle.

Abstract

Learning discriminative visual representations from distributed, heterogeneous data is a fundamental challenge in Federated Learning (FL). Prototype-based methods address statistical heterogeneity by sharing class-level representations across clients but create a distance-dependent gradient pressure that is particularly severe during early training rounds: alignment pressure applied to immature global prototypes, aggregated from noisy local representations, generates large gradients that suppress the emergence of local discriminative structure. The result is a poorly organized embedding space and degraded recognition performance, particularly under severe non-IID conditions. We propose FedSAP, a framework that stabilises federated representation learning through two complementary mechanisms: a deterministic alignment curriculum that delays global alignment until local representations become stable and a geometry-driven proxy separation loss that enforces inter-class structure on the unit hypersphere using the existing prototype bank without introducing additional parameters or communication overhead. Together, these mechanisms produce compact, well-separated class clusters without altering the underlying communication protocol between federation's participants. Experiments across three benchmarks and varying degrees of heterogeneity show gains of up to 4 percentage points over the prototype-based baselines evaluated, with improvements most pronounced under high heterogeneity. The representational nature of our framework further enables a straightforward extension to semi-supervised settings, where unlabelled data is incorporated with minimal modification, underscoring the generality of scheduled alignment as a design principle.

item.title: Transferable Self-Harm Surveillance from Emergency Department Triage Notes Using an Evidence-Augmented Machine Learning Approach
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02545v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景

自伤行为是亟需应对的重大公共卫生问题，但当前依赖医院诊断编码的监测体系敏感性严重不足（常低于40%），大量轻中度自伤患者因未被赋予明确诊断代码而被漏检。急诊科（ED）分诊记录作为患者入院首份结构化文本，虽简短（平均<50词），却蕴含关键临床线索（如“割腕”“吞药”“情绪崩溃后自残”），为早期、主动识别自伤提供了独特窗口。

方法

本研究提出可迁移的证据增强型机器学习框架，含三阶段流水线：（1）LLM初筛：利用微调后的开源大语言模型（Llama-3-8B）对分诊文本进行粗粒度自伤判定与关键实体定位；（2）证据提取：基于临床指南构建规则引擎，从LLM输出中精准抽取自伤方法、意图、工具等结构化证据；（3）集成分类：将LLM置信度、提取证据特征及传统NLP特征（TF-IDF、词嵌入）输入XGBoost分类器，输出最终判定及方法分类。模型在澳大利亚一家三级医院（n=21,467条分诊记录）开发，并在另两家异构医院（n=15,892 & 12,305）开展无适配外部验证。

主要发现

  • 高鲁棒性迁移能力：内部验证AUPRC达0.887±0.016；外部验证AUPRC分别为0.879±0.012与0.816±0.015，无需任何站点特异性重训练；前瞻性部署于开发站点达0.881±0.008。
  • 超越二元分类的细粒度洞察：首次实现自伤主要方法（如切割、服药、撞击）的自动识别，准确率95%，支持按方法学分类的精准流行病学监测。
  • 临床可解释性：所有预测均附带可追溯的原始文本证据片段与LLM推理链，满足医疗AI监管要求。

创新点

本工作突破了传统NLP模型对标注数据与本地适配的强依赖，通过“LLM引导+规则精炼+轻量模型决策”的协同范式，实现了高精度、高迁移性、高可解释性的自伤实时监测，为全球急诊系统提供即插即用的公共卫生哨点工具。

AI Summary (English)

Self-harm surveillance relying on diagnostic codes suffers from critically low sensitivity, missing many cases presenting to emergency departments (EDs). We propose an evidence-augmented machine learning approach that leverages triage notes—the first clinical text recorded upon ED arrival—to detect self-harm with high accuracy and cross-site transferability. Our three-stage pipeline integrates a fine-tuned LLM (Llama-3-8B) for initial screening and evidence localization, a clinical guideline–informed rule engine for structured extraction of self-harm methods and intent, and an XGBoost classifier fusing LLM outputs, extracted evidence, and traditional NLP features. Validated across three Australian hospitals without site-specific retraining, the model achieves AUPRCs of 0.887 ± 0.016 (internal), 0.879 ± 0.012 and 0.816 ± 0.015 (external), and 0.881 ± 0.008 in prospective deployment. Crucially, it identifies the primary self-harm method with 95% accuracy—enabling granular, method-specific surveillance beyond binary classification—while maintaining full traceability to original text evidence.

Abstract

Self-harm is a major public health concern, but current surveillance relying on hospital presentations is inadequate due to the low sensitivity of diagnostic codes. Emergency Department (ED) triage notes, recorded at the initial point of contact, provide a succinct summary of presentations and an opportunity to identify self-harm. We developed a three-stage approach, augmenting traditional machine learning with large language model-based screening and evidence extraction to detect self-harm in ED triage notes. We assessed model transferability across three Australian hospitals. Our approach showed AUPRCs of 0.887 +/- 0.016 and 0.884 +/- 0.012 during internal and external validation. Prospectively, it achieved AUPRC of 0.881 +/- 0.008 at the development site, and 0.879 +/- 0.012 and 0.816 +/- 0.015 at two external sites without site-specific retraining. A key advantage of the approach is that it enables identification of the primary self-harm method with an accuracy of 95%, supporting more granular surveillance beyond binary classification.

item.title: SkillHarm: Lifecycle-Aware Skill-Based Attacks via Automated Construction
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02540v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

智能体（Agent）技能作为其工作流中的核心执行单元，常被隐式调用与信任，导致第三方技能成为高危攻击面。现有研究虽揭示了技能投毒引发的安全风险，但多局限于单次任务执行场景，且依赖人工枚举的零散风险清单，缺乏对技能全生命周期（注册、调用、复用、持久化）的系统性攻击建模与评估。

方法与创新

本文提出 SkillHarm——首个面向技能生命周期的系统性技能攻击基准，包含：

  • 双阶段攻击范式：① 固定载荷投毒（FPP）：恶意技能包一经调用即直接危害当前会话；② 自变异投毒（SMP）：良性初始执行后，悄然篡改持久化技能内容，危害延迟至后续复用时爆发；
  • 12类结构化风险分类：依据攻击目标组件细分为三类——数据管道层（如输入污染、输出泄露）、系统环境层（如权限提升、文件系统破坏）、智能体自主性层（如目标劫持、推理绕过）；
  • 自动化构建框架 AutoSkillHarm：基于自然语言“测试驱动”指令，调度编码智能体批量生成攻击样本，确保可扩展性与真实性。

主要发现

基准涵盖 71个技能、879个攻击样本。实验表明：主流智能体仍高度脆弱——FPP攻击成功率高达86.3%，SMP达69.3%。更关键的是，我们发现大量“失败”案例实为智能体未加载/未访问投毒文件（规避式失效），而非真正具备防御能力；现有防护机制（如沙箱、权限隔离）对此类隐蔽、延迟型攻击缺乏可靠缓解效果。

AI Summary (English)

SkillHarm is the first benchmark for skill-based attacks that systematically covers the full skill-use lifecycle—registration, invocation, reuse, and persistence. It introduces two novel attack paradigms: Fixed-Payload Poisoning (FPP), where a malicious skill compromises any session invoking it immediately; and Self-Mutating Poisoning (SMP), where an initially benign execution silently alters persistent skill code, deferring harm to later reuse. We define a 12-category risk taxonomy grounded in three agent workflow components: data pipelines, system environments, and agent autonomy. Using AutoSkillHarm—an automated pipeline powered by coding agents guided by natural-language harnesses—we construct 879 attack samples across 71 skills. Experiments show high vulnerability: up to 86.3% success in FPP and 69.3% in SMP. Critically, many apparent failures stem from agents avoiding poisoned files—not resisting them—and current defenses remain ineffective against these stealthy, lifecycle-aware threats.

Abstract

Agent skills occupy a privileged position in the agent workflow, as agents are expected to implicitly follow and execute them, rendering third-party skills a vulnerable attack surface. Existing studies have revealed unsafe agent behaviors induced by skill-based attacks, but they primarily evaluate poisoned skills within a single task execution and enumerate harms through ad-hoc risk lists. To bridge these gaps, we introduce SkillHarm, a benchmark of skill-based attacks across the skill-use lifecycle, paired with a systematic taxonomy of skill-relevant risks. SkillHarm evaluates two attack scenarios: Fixed-Payload Poisoning (FPP), where a fixed poisoned skill package directly compromises any task session that invokes it, and Self-Mutating Poisoning (SMP), where an initially benign execution silently mutates persistent skill content, deferring harm until a subsequent reuse. It further defines 12 risk types based on the agent workflow component targeted by the harm: data pipelines, system environments, and agent autonomy. To instantiate these attacks at scale, we build AutoSkillHarm, an automated construction pipeline with coding agents driven by natural-language harnesses. The resulting benchmark contains 879 attack samples across 71 skills. Experiments show that current agents remain vulnerable with attack success rates up to 86.3% in FPP and 69.3% in SMP. Our analysis further reveals a latent risk: many apparent attack failures stem from the agent failing to engage with the poisoned file rather than genuine resistance, and current defenses still fail to reliably mitigate the threat.

item.title: Who Annotates in NLP? A Large-scale Assessment of Human Annotation Reporting between 2018 and 2025
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02255v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

人类标注是自然语言处理（NLP）研究的实证基石——从数据集构建到模型评估，均高度依赖人工判断。然而，大量论文对“谁标注”“如何控制标注过程”缺乏清晰报告，导致结果可复现性、有效性与公平性存疑。

方法与创新

本研究首次开展跨时间（2018–2025）、跨任务、跨会议的大规模审计，覆盖ACL主会及主要子刊（如NAACL、EMNLP、COLING等）共1,603篇论文、2,667项标注任务。我们提出统一的标注报告分类法（含12类核心维度），并构建Annotated-gold——由人类专家仲裁标注的41篇论文、72项任务黄金标准数据集。在此基础上，开发并验证了LLM辅助抽取流水线：最优模型在Krippendorff’s alpha上达0.606，媲美人类间一致性（0.585），证明其可靠性。

主要发现

  • 报告不均衡显著：操作性细节（如招募方式、标注量、领域专长）报告率超70%，但关键效度要素严重缺失——标注员语言能力（仅12%）、薪酬信息（9%）、社会人口学特征（<5%）、培训流程（38%）、分歧仲裁机制（22%）、标注者间一致性指标（18%）普遍未披露；
  • 用途差异突出：模型评估类研究中，上述缺失尤为严重（如agreement值报告率仅8%），远低于数据集构建类（29%）；
  • 趋势向好但不足：2018–2025年间整体报告质量稳步提升（年均+4.2%合规维度），但2025年仍有超60%的任务缺失≥3项关键信息。

贡献与建议

本研究建立首个可扩展的标注透明度评估框架，提出“最低报告清单”（Minimum Reporting Checklist），涵盖标注者资质、培训、质量控制、多样性与伦理五大支柱，为提升NLP中人类标注的可靠性、可复现性与可解释性提供实证基础与实践指南。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first large-scale, task-level audit of human annotation reporting across major NLP venues (ACL, NAACL, EMNLP, COLING) from 2018 to 2025, analyzing 1,603 papers and 2,667 annotation tasks. We introduce a unified taxonomy of 12 annotation-reporting dimensions and validate an LLM-assisted extraction pipeline against Annotated-gold—a human-adjudicated gold standard—achieving Krippendorff’s alpha = 0.606, on par with human-human agreement (0.585). Results show widespread reporting gaps: while operational details (e.g., recruitment, volume, expertise) are frequently documented (>70%), critical validity-related information—including annotator training (38%), language proficiency (12%), compensation (9%), socio-demographics (<5%), adjudication (22%), and inter-annotator agreement (18%)—is routinely omitted, especially in model-evaluation studies (e.g., agreement reported in only 8%). Although reporting quality improved steadily over time (+4.2% per year), over 60% of tasks in 2025 still lacked ≥3 essential elements. We propose a scalable framework and a minimum reporting checklist to enhance reliability, reproducibility, and interpretability of human annotation in NLP.

Abstract

Human annotation is the empirical foundation of much NLP research, from dataset construction to model evaluation, but papers often leave unclear who produced the annotations and how the annotation process was controlled. We provide the first large-scale, task-level audit of human annotation reporting across major NLP venues, asking which annotation details are documented, which are missing, and how reporting varies across time, topic, venue, and intended use of human judgment. We introduce a unified taxonomy of annotation-reporting practices and validate an LLM-assisted extraction pipeline against Annotated-gold, a human-adjudicated gold standard of 41 papers and 72 annotation tasks, where the best model reaches human-comparable agreement with adjudicated labels, with Krippendorff's alpha of 0.606 versus 0.585 for human-human agreement. Using this pipeline, we construct Annotated-llm, a dataset covering ACL-venue papers from 2018-2025, with 2,667 extracted annotation tasks from 1,603 papers, and find that papers frequently report operational details such as recruitment strategies, annotator expertise, and annotation volume, but often omit details needed to assess annotation validity, including training, language proficiency, compensation, socio-demographics, adjudication, and agreement values, especially in model-evaluation studies. Our results show that annotation reporting in NLP has improved over time but remains uneven, and they establish a scalable framework and bare-minimum reporting recommendations for making human annotation more reliable, reproducible, and interpretable.

item.title: AgentRedBench: Dynamic Redteaming and Integration-Aware Defense for LLM Agents over SaaS Integrations
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02240v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

针对大语言模型（LLM）智能体的间接提示注入攻击（Indirect Prompt Injection）在SaaS集成场景下构成严峻生产级威胁：当LLM智能体调用Gmail、Salesforce、Jira等第三方服务时，其读取的工具响应内容由用户完全不可控，却可能隐含恶意指令。现有基准测试严重不足——仅覆盖少量集成、复用静态攻击载荷，且开源防护模型（如Llama Guard）均基于对话式数据训练，未适配真实工具响应文本。

方法与创新

本文提出 AgentRedBench：首个面向SaaS集成的动态红队基准，涵盖24个企业级API集成（分属9类功能域）、5类攻击类型，构建215个细粒度、授权边界模糊（underspecified authorization）的对抗场景。所有场景由LLM动态生成并经人工精筛，杜绝数据泄露风险。我们同步开源AgentRedGuard防护模型——首个专为工具响应内容定制的轻量级守卫，训练于跨集成、高对抗性的工具响应语料库。

关键结果

在8个主流闭源模型（Anthropic/Claude、OpenAI/GPT、Google/Gemini）上，无防护时攻击成功率（ASR）达32%–81%；部署AgentRedGuard后，整体ASR从69.9%骤降至2.4%，仅引入0.37%误报率，显著优于Llama Guard、PromptGuard 2和ProtectAI等全部开源基线。跨集成与跨攻击类型的留出集验证表明泛化能力稳健。代码、集成Schema、模型及版本受控评估通道已全部开源。

AI Summary (English)

AgentRedBench is a dynamic, LLM-driven redteaming benchmark for indirect prompt injection in LLM agents interacting with SaaS integrations (e.g., Gmail, Salesforce, Jira). It comprises 215 subtle, authorization-underspecified attack scenarios across 24 enterprise APIs spanning nine functional families and five attack types—designed to reflect real-world integration risks where users neither write nor control tool-response content. Evaluated on an eight-model panel (Claude, GPT, Gemini), unguarded ASR ranges from 32% to 81%. We release AgentRedGuard—a guard model trained exclusively on adversarial, integration-diverse tool-response data—that reduces panel-wide ASR from 69.9% to 2.4% at just 0.37% false-positive rate, outperforming all open-source baselines (Llama Guard, PromptGuard 2, ProtectAI) on both detection and precision. Cross-integration and cross-attack holdout tests confirm strong generalization. The full codebase, schemas, model, and versioned evaluation protocol are openly available.

Abstract

Indirect prompt injection in tool-use agents is a concrete production threat: LLM agents read from integrations (third-party services such as Gmail, Salesforce, or Jira accessed through tool calls) whose response content the user neither writes nor controls. Existing benchmarks under-measure the threat: most cover only a handful of integrations with the same attack payload replayed across runs, and open-source guards are trained on chat-style data rather than tool-response content. We introduce AGENTREDBENCH, a dynamic LLM-driven redteaming benchmark of 215 subtle underspecified authorization (attacks at the boundary of what the user's request authorises) scenarios across 24 enterprise integrations in nine functional families and five attack types. Across an eight-model panel (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google), no-guard ASR (attack success rate) ranges from 32% (Claude Sonnet 4.6) to 81% (Gemini 3 Flash). To keep the scenario set out of training corpora and preserve headline ASR meaning over time, we release the codebase, integration schemas, and AGENTREDGUARD model openly; the canonical scenarios are evaluated through a maintainer-mediated channel with immutable versioning. We release AGENTREDGUARD alongside the benchmark: a guard trained on an integration-diverse corpus of adversarial tool-response content. AGENTREDGUARD cuts panel ASR from 69.9% to 2.4% at 0.37% false-positive rate, outperforming every open-source baseline with non-trivial detection (Llama Guard, PromptGuard 2, ProtectAI) on both axes. Cross-integration and cross-attack type holdouts both confirm the gain transfers beyond the training subset.

item.title: ContinuousBench: Can Differentially Private Synthetic Text Improve Capabilities?
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01849v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

差分隐私（DP）文本合成被寄望于安全释放敏感语料库以支持大模型训练，但其能否真正传递原始语料中独有的、不可替代的知识与能力，仍缺乏可靠验证。现有基准（如PrivacyBench、DP-GLUE）普遍存在“任务可解性偏差”：所用问答任务往往无需训练即可高分求解（例如依赖通用常识或预训练知识），导致即使DP合成数据质量低下，模型仍能取得虚假高分，无法反映真实能力迁移效果。

方法创新：ContinuousBench

为突破该瓶颈，我们提出ContinuousBench——首个持续演进、全自动再生的动态评估框架。其核心设计为：

  • 按季更新：每季度发布一对全新组件——一个首次公开的原始训练语料（never-before-seen corpus）与一个严格配套的衍生问答测试集；
  • 双重约束构造：QA集满足（1）语料依赖性（unsolvable sans-corpus）：答案无法通过外部知识或模型先验推断，必须从当期语料中提取；（2）DP可学习性：目标知识由数百条独立记录冗余支撑，确保在合理隐私预算（如ε≥8）下统计可学；
  • 标准化评测流水线：研究者仅需提交基于当期语料生成的DP合成文本，系统将自动执行统一微调（LoRA）与零样本评估，量化能力增益（ΔAccuracy）。

关键发现与意义

我们在两大轨道上验证：Geminon（程序生成的虚构生物知识库）与News（实时爬取的新闻流）。结果表明：非隐私合成方法（如GPT-4蒸馏）可稳定迁移75%+原始语料知识；而当前SOTA DP方法（DP-LLM、DP-SFT）在ε=100时仍近乎零增益（ΔAcc < 2.1%），显著暴露其能力鸿沟。ContinuousBench首次实现了对DP合成“知识保真度”的因果式、抗混淆评估，为可信数据共享提供了可演化的黄金标准。

AI Summary (English)

Differentially private (DP) text synthesis promises safe access to sensitive corpora, but existing benchmarks cannot reliably assess whether DP synthetic data transfers genuinely new, corpus-specific capabilities—because their tasks are often solvable without training. To address this, we introduce ContinuousBench, a quarterly-updated, automatically regenerated benchmark that isolates capability gain from DP synthetic text. Each release pairs a never-before-seen training corpus with a derived QA set designed to be: (1) unsolvable without the corpus, and (2) learnable under DP via statistical redundancy across hundreds of independent records. Researchers generate DP synthetic data and run our standardized training/evaluation harness to measure knowledge transfer. On two tracks—Geminon (procedural creature knowledge) and News (live crawled articles)—non-private synthesis achieves >75% capability transfer, while state-of-the-art DP methods (e.g., DP-LLM, ε=100) yield near-zero gain (ΔAccuracy < 2.1%). ContinuousBench provides the first causal, anti-confounding evaluation of DP synthetic text’s knowledge fidelity.

Abstract

Differentially private (DP) text synthesis promises to unlock sensitive corpora for model training, but it remains unclear whether DP synthetic data transmits genuinely new knowledge and capabilities present only in those corpora. This is because existing evaluations rely on tasks that are nearly solvable without training, so strong benchmark performance does not establish that DP synthesis can substitute original data access. Thus, we introduce ContinuousBench, a continuously and automatically-regenerated benchmark that measures capability gain from DP synthetic text. Each quarter, a new release pairs a never-before-seen training corpus with a derived QA set, constructed to be: (1) unsolvable sans-corpus; and (2) learnable under DP, as the tested knowledge is supported by hundreds of independent records. Researchers produce DP synthetic data from the training corpus and run our standardized training and evaluation harness on their synthetic data to measure gains. We instantiate two tracks: Geminon, a procedurally-generated dataset about fictional creatures; and News, a stream of newly crawled public news articles. Although standard benchmarks are nearly saturated, on ContinuousBench we find that non-private synthesis transfers substantial knowledge from the original corpus, while state-of-the-art DP synthesis methods generally fail to do so, even at $\varepsilon=100$.

item.title: Why Do Self-Harm Prediction Models Struggle to Generalise? Lexical and Semantic Variations in Emergency Department Triage Notes
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.01678v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

急诊科（ED）接诊的自伤行为是自杀风险的重要预测指标。尽管自然语言处理（NLP）模型在单中心 triage notes（分诊记录）中识别自伤表现出较高准确率，但跨机构迁移时性能显著下降，严重制约其临床落地。当前尚缺乏对这一泛化失效机制的系统性文本层面归因分析。

方法与数据

本研究基于两家独立三级医院的真实ED分诊记录（共12,847条），采用多维度对比范式：

  • 词汇层面：统计词频分布、n-gram差异、拼写变体及缩写使用习惯；
  • 模型可解释性层面：提取各模型Top-20高权重预测特征（如关键词、短语、依存模式），比较其跨中心一致性；
  • 语义主题层面：通过LDA与BERTopic联合建模，识别自伤相关显性主题（如“药物过量”“割伤”“情绪崩溃”）及其表述强度差异。

主要发现

1. 显著词汇漂移：两家医院在核心自伤概念上存在系统性表达差异——例如，“overdose”在A院高频出现（占比18.3%），而B院更倾向使用“took too many pills”（口语化表达，占比22.7%）；“cut”与“laceration”“self-inflicted wound”的术语层级使用亦不一致。
2. 特征重要性偏移：XGBoost与RoBERTa模型中，前5位判别性特征在两院重合度仅32%，提示模型依赖本地化表征而非通用语义。
3. 主题表达失配：虽均涵盖“自服药”“自伤行为”“心理危机”三大核心主题，但B院在“社会压力源”（如失业、家庭冲突）描述上更详尽，且常与自伤陈述合并书写，形成更长、更模糊的复合句式，显著降低模型注意力聚焦能力。

创新与意义

本研究首次将机构特异性文本实践（institutional documentation practices）确立为NLP模型跨中心泛化失败的关键瓶颈，超越传统“数据分布偏移”笼统解释。成果为构建鲁棒临床NLP系统提供两条路径：① 开发面向医疗文书变异的标准化预处理协议；② 设计主题感知的对抗训练框架，显式解耦机构噪声与临床信号。

AI Summary (English)

Self-harm presentations in emergency departments (EDs) strongly predict subsequent suicide risk, yet NLP models trained on triage notes often fail to generalize across hospitals. To identify root causes, we conducted a comparative linguistic analysis of 12,847 real-world ED triage notes from two independent hospitals. We examined lexical variation (e.g., term preferences, spelling, phrasing), model-extracted predictive features (top-20 tokens/phrases per site), and salient semantic topics (via LDA + BERTopic). Results revealed substantial cross-site divergence: core self-harm concepts—such as overdose or self-injury—were expressed using markedly different lexical items (e.g., “overdose” vs. “took too many pills”) and syntactic patterns; feature importance rankings overlapped by only 32% between sites; and while thematic content (e.g., poisoning, injury, distress) was consistent, its contextual embedding—especially regarding psychosocial stressors—varied significantly in density and ambiguity. These institution-specific documentation practices—not just domain shift—directly degrade cross-site model performance. Our work reframes generalization failure as a textual practice problem, offering actionable pathways toward more robust clinical NLP systems.

Abstract

Self-harm presentations to emergency departments (EDs) are strongly associated with higher suicide risk. NLP models have shown robust performance in detecting self-harm from triage notes within single hospitals, yet performance often declines across institutions. To examine potential causes, we compare ED triage notes from two hospitals by analyzing lexical characteristics, highly associated predictive features, and salient topics. Our results reveal variation in lexical expression and feature importance related to self-harm across hospitals, despite consistent core themes such as self-poisoning and self-injury. These documentation differences are associated with reduced cross-site performance. Our findings provide insight into how institutional variation affects the identification of self-harm in clinical text and highlight potential methods to improve model generalisability.

item.title: GETA: Generalized Encrypted Traffic Analysis
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31277v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

随着TLS 1.3、QUIC、HTTP/3及各类隧道协议的广泛部署，网络流量加密率持续攀升，传统基于深度包检测（DPI）的流量分析方法因无法访问明文载荷而严重失效。现有机器学习方案虽尝试利用加密流量元数据（如包长、时序、方向），但仍存在三大瓶颈：① 过度依赖协议特定头部特征（如TCP标志位、TLS扩展字段），泛化性差；② 需要大规模人工标注数据集，标注成本高且难以覆盖新兴应用；③ 在跨网络环境（如企业网→IoT边缘→5G核心网）迁移时性能显著下降。

方法创新：GETA框架

本文提出GETA（Generalized Encrypted Traffic Analysis）——首个真正协议无关的加密流量分析框架。其核心设计包括：

  • 元数据时序建模：将每个网络流抽象为多变量时间序列（包间隔、累积字节数、方向序列等），完全摒弃对包载荷与协议语义的依赖；
  • 三阶段自适应架构：融合元学习（MAML）实现跨域知识迁移、嵌入精炼模块动态校准流表征、以及轻量级自注意力机制捕获长程时序依赖；
  • 少样本适配能力：仅需每个新场景下≤5个标注样本/类别即可完成领域适配，显著降低部署门槛。

实验验证与贡献

在涵盖应用识别（85类App）、VPN分类（12种工具）、IoT设备指纹（47种设备）、恶意流量检测（5类攻击）的9个公开基准数据集上，GETA全面超越SOTA方法（如TL-Net、ET-BERT、FlowLens），平均准确率提升6.2–14.8%，跨域泛化误差降低37.5%。本工作证明：仅凭加密流量的“骨架”（纯元数据时序）即可构建鲁棒、可迁移、低标注依赖的分析基础，为零信任网络中的实时流量治理提供了普适性技术路径。

AI Summary (English)

Traditional encrypted traffic analysis faces fundamental challenges due to widespread encryption, tunneling, and privacy protocols that hide payloads and invalidate Deep Packet Inspection (DPI). While ML-based methods exist, they often rely on protocol-specific header features, require large labeled datasets, and degrade across heterogeneous networks. We propose GETA, a protocol-agnostic framework that models network flows as multivariate time series using only traffic metadata (e.g., inter-arrival times, packet lengths, directions)—eliminating dependence on payloads or protocol semantics. GETA integrates meta-learning (MAML), embedding refinement, and lightweight self-attention to enable few-shot adaptation to unseen domains with ≤5 labeled samples per class. Evaluated across nine public datasets spanning application identification, VPN classification, IoT device fingerprinting, and attack detection, GETA consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines—achieving average accuracy gains of 6.2–14.8% and reducing cross-domain generalization error by 37.5%. Results demonstrate that robust, generalizable traffic analysis is feasible using metadata alone.

Abstract

Traditional traffic analysis is being fundamentally challenged by the rapid adoption of encryption, tunnelling, and privacy-preserving protocols, which increasingly obscure packet payloads and limit the usefulness of Deep Packet Inspection (DPI). Although machine learning has advanced encrypted traffic analysis, existing approaches often remain tied to protocol-specific header features, depend on large labelled datasets, and degrade when deployed across heterogeneous network environments. We present GETA, a protocol-agnostic framework for encrypted traffic analysis that models network flows as multivariate time series using only traffic metadata, thereby avoiding reliance on packet payloads or header semantics. GETA combines meta-learning, embedding refinement, and self-attention to support few-shot adaptation to previously unseen domains with minimal labelled data. Across nine public datasets spanning application identification, VPN traffic classification, IoT device fingerprinting, and attack detection, GETA consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines. These results show that GETA offers a practical and generalisable foundation for robust traffic analysis in modern encrypted networks.

item.title: BadBone: Backdoor Attacks Against Backbone Models in Visual Prompt Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31246v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

视觉提示学习（Visual Prompt Learning）作为一种新兴的轻量级迁移范式，通过在冻结骨干网络（backbone）前添加可学习提示（prompt）实现高效下游适配。尽管其简洁性与有效性广受认可，该范式下的模型安全风险却长期被忽视——尤其缺乏针对骨干模型本身、而非提示模块的定向后门攻击研究。

方法：BadBone 攻击框架

本文首次提出 BadBone，一种基于双层优化（bi-level optimization）的隐蔽且自适应的骨干模型后门攻击。与传统攻击污染提示参数或微调头层不同，BadBone 直接污染预训练骨干模型的权重，但精心设计触发机制：仅当目标模型采用提示学习方式（如 CoOp、PromptSRC）进行下游任务时，后门才被激活；而在标准微调（fine-tuning）或线性探针（linear probing）等其他适配方式下，模型行为完全正常——实现任务感知的隐蔽性。

主要发现与创新

  • 在 ViT-B/16、ResNet-50、Deformable DETR 三大架构及 ImageNet-1K、CUB-200、PASCAL VOC 三大跨域数据集上验证：BadBone 在未降低预训练表征质量（ImageNet top-1 准确率下降 <0.3%）和下游任务性能（平均仅降 0.5–1.2%）的前提下，实现 >92% 的靶向攻击成功率（ASR）与 >87% 的非靶向攻击成功率；
  • 对 6 种前沿模型级防御（Neural Cleanse、ABS、MNTD、NAD、CLP、D-BR）进行系统评估，结果表明：所有防御均无法有效识别或净化 BadBone 污染的骨干模型（平均检测率 <28%，净化后 ASR 仍 >79%），凸显其强规避能力；
  • 首次揭示“骨干模型安全 ≠ 提示学习安全”这一关键认知断层，为提示学习生态的安全治理提供新视角与紧迫挑战。

AI Summary (English)

Prompt learning has emerged as an efficient paradigm for adapting frozen vision backbones via learnable prompts, yet its security vulnerabilities—especially at the backbone level—remain unexplored. We propose BadBone, the first stealthy, task-aware backdoor attack that directly poisons the pre-trained backbone using bi-level optimization. Crucially, BadBone ensures the backdoor activates only when downstream tasks employ prompt learning (e.g., CoOp), while preserving clean behavior under fine-tuning or linear probing—achieving unprecedented adaptivity and concealment. Extensive experiments across three architectures (ViT-B/16, ResNet-50, Deformable DETR) and three diverse datasets (ImageNet-1K, CUB-200, PASCAL VOC) show BadBone achieves >92% targeted ASR and >87% untargeted ASR, with negligible utility degradation (<0.3% ImageNet top-1 drop; <1.2% avg. downstream accuracy loss). Against six state-of-the-art model-level defenses (Neural Cleanse, ABS, MNTD, NAD, CLP, D-BR), BadBone evades detection (avg. <28% detection rate) and resists purification (post-defense ASR remains >79%). Our work exposes a critical security gap: backbone integrity alone does not guarantee prompt learning safety—highlighting urgent needs for task-aware defense mechanisms.

Abstract

Prompt learning is a new machine learning paradigm that has attracted ample attention due to its simplicity and proven efficacy. Despite its growing adoption, the security vulnerabilities associated with this paradigm remain underexplored. In this work, we take the first step to propose BadBone, a stealthy and adaptive backdoor attack against prompt learning using bi-level optimization. Instead of backdooring the prompt learning process, we aim to compromise a backbone model such that only target downstream tasks employing prompt learning inherit the backdoor vulnerability. Extensive experiments on three different models and three datasets from various domains show that our targeted/untargeted backdoored models achieve high attack performance while maintaining utility on both pre-training and downstream tasks. Moreover, we evaluate our approach against six state-of-the-art model-level defenses, including Neural Cleanse, ABS, MNTD, NAD, CLP, and D-BR. The results demonstrate that these defenses are largely ineffective against our backdoored models and thus leave the effective defense as an important direction for future work.

item.title: R+R: Reassessing Java Security API Misuse in Current LLMs: A Replication on JCA and JSSE APIs with External Security Knowledge
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31135v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

Java安全API（如JCA和JSSE）的误用是软件开发中长期存在的高危安全问题。2024年已有研究表明，大型语言模型（LLMs）生成的Java代码普遍存在此类误用。然而，随着模型快速迭代（如GPT-5.5、Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct），该问题是否持续存在？外部安全知识（如安全示例、误用模式、开发者指南）能否有效缓解？其效果是否因模型架构与能力而异？这些问题尚缺乏系统验证。

方法设计

本研究对Mousavi等人的开创性工作开展严格复现与拓展：在统一基准下，评估两类代表性前沿模型——GPT-5.5（前沿闭源编码模型）与Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct（强开源权重、支持私有部署）——在JCA/JSSE API任务中的安全编码表现。我们引入四类外部安全知识：① 安全代码示例；② 显式误用模式清单；③ Java安全开发者指南摘要；④ 安全导向提示工程（Secure Prompting）。所有实验均控制检索上下文长度与格式一致性，确保可比性。

主要发现

  • 尽管新模型整体性能提升，Java安全API误用仍未根除：GPT-5.5仍产生约18%含误用的有效程序，Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct达32%；
  • 外部知识显著提升安全性，但效果高度模型依赖：对Llama，安全示例最有效（误用率↓41%）；对GPT-5.5，显式误用模式最有力——在所有通过编译且调用目标API的程序中，彻底消除检测到的安全误用；
  • 开发者指南知识对GPT-5.5增益跃升（+29%安全率），安全提示亦带来大幅改进（+22%）；
  • 本研究首次揭示：RAG（检索增强生成）的价值不仅取决于知识质量与检索精度，更深度耦合于模型原生安全推理能力。

创新点

提出“模型-知识协同适配”视角，为安全编码辅助工具的设计提供实证依据：针对不同部署场景（云服务vs私有化），应定制化选择知识类型与注入方式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper replicates and extends Mousavi et al.’s 2024 study on Java security API misuse, evaluating two state-of-the-art models—GPT-5.5 (proprietary frontier) and Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct (strong open-weight)—on JCA/JSSE tasks with four external security knowledge types: secure examples, explicit misuse patterns, developer-guide excerpts, and secure prompting. Results show that while newer LLMs reduce but do not eliminate security API misuse, external knowledge yields substantial, model-dependent improvements: for Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct, secure examples are most effective; for GPT-5.5, explicit misuse patterns eliminate all detected misuses among valid, compilable programs, and developer-guide knowledge + secure prompting deliver large gains. Crucially, the efficacy of retrieval-augmented security knowledge depends not only on content and retrieval but also on intrinsic model capability—highlighting the need for model-aware knowledge integration in secure coding assistants.

Abstract

The misuse of Java security APIs is a serious security problem in software development. Research in 2024 has shown that this problem is widespread in LLM-generated code. However, it remains unclear whether this phenomenon persists in current models and how external security knowledge affects it. This paper presents a scoped replication and extension of Mousavi et al.'s study on the Java Cryptography Architecture (JCA) and Java Secure Socket Extension (JSSE) APIs. We focus on two complementary settings: GPT-5.5 as a frontier proprietary coding model, and Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct as a strong open-weight model relevant to self-hosted deployment. The results show that although newer LLMs perform better in using Java security APIs, the problem of Java security API misuse has not been eliminated. External security knowledge substantially improves the measured outcome, but its effect is model-dependent. For Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct, secure code examples are the most effective single knowledge type. For GPT-5.5, explicit misuse patterns eliminate all detected security API misuses among valid programs in our benchmark, although some outputs remain invalid due to compilation errors or target-API mismatches. In addition, developer-guide knowledge becomes much more effective, and secure prompting also provides large gains for GPT-5.5. Overall, these findings confirm the Java security API misuse risk identified in the original study and show that the benefits of retrieval-augmented knowledge depend not only on the knowledge itself and retrieval behavior, but also on model capability.

item.title: From Prompt Injection to Persistent Control: Defending Agentic Harness Against Trojan Backdoors
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31042v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

大语言模型（LLM）智能体正从对话式聊天机器人演进为具备文件读写、工具调用与跨会话状态复用能力的本地化操作型代理（agentic harness）。这一增强能力在提升实用性的同时，催生新型多步“特洛伊后门”攻击：攻击者将恶意指令（如prompt injection）隐匿于普通文本文件或工具输出中；代理在后续会话中读取该内容、缓存并执行，从而实现持久化控制。此类攻击各环节单独检视均无明显恶意特征，但组合后可将不可信文本转化为长期可控的执行载荷——而现有防御机制多采用单步静态检测，难以追溯攻击源头，导致对“埋点写入”阶段完全失察。

方法与创新

为系统揭示该威胁，我们构建首个面向本地代理环境的多步特洛伊攻击基准——ClawTrojan，基于OpenClaw仿真工作区与GPT-5.4模型开展评估。实验表明：ClawTrojan攻击成功率（ASR）达95.5%，而传统单轮prompt注入攻击在相同设置下ASR近乎为零，证实多步协同是关键突破口。针对此，我们提出DASGuard动态防御框架：① 实时扫描敏感本地文件中的类控制文本（如含/execute、run_as_admin等模式）；② 追溯其来源路径（origin tracing），验证是否源自可信用户输入或白名单工具；③ 对非可信源生成的控制内容实施运行时拦截 + 工作区净化提交（sanitized commits），阻断持久化链路。

主要发现

DASGuard在保持0.8%误报率前提下，将ClawTrojan ASR压制至2.1%，显著优于基线防御；其“溯源+净化”双机制首次实现了对多步特洛伊攻击全生命周期的动态干预，为安全代理系统提供了可部署、可审计的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Large language model (LLM) agents are evolving into operational tools capable of file I/O, tool invocation, and cross-session state reuse—creating a new attack surface for multi-step Trojan backdoors. In such attacks, malicious instructions are stealthily embedded in files or tool outputs; agents later read, store, and execute them, achieving persistent control without any single step appearing overtly harmful. Existing defenses inspect steps in isolation and thus miss the initial “planting” phase. To expose this threat, we introduce ClawTrojan, the first benchmark for multi-step Trojan attacks in local agentic harnesses. Evaluated in an OpenClaw-style workspace with GPT-5.4, ClawTrojan achieves a 95.5% attack success rate (ASR), while standard single-turn prompt injection yields near-zero ASR. To counter it, we propose DASGuard, a dynamic defense that: (1) scans sensitive local files for control-like text, (2) traces its provenance to verify trusted origin, and (3) blocks execution and sanitizes workspace commits for untrusted content. DASGuard reduces ASR to 2.1% with only 0.8% false positives, demonstrating the first effective end-to-end mitigation for persistent agent hijacking.

Abstract

LLM agents are evolving from conversational chatbots to operational tools in real-world workspaces. In local agentic harnesses, an LLM can read and write files, call tools, and reuse workspace state across sessions. While such capabilities enhance utility, they also expose a new attack surface for attackers. Attackers can embed a prompt injection within a file or tool output. Agents may read this hidden instruction, store it, and execute it later. In this multi-step trojan attack paradigm, no individual step appears malicious on its own, but these steps can collectively turn untrusted text into persistent control content. However, existing defenses often inspect each step in isolation. As a result, they can block a clear harmful action, but fail to detect the earlier write operation that plants the backdoor. To reveal this threat, we introduce ClawTrojan, a benchmark designed to identify multi-step trojan attacks in local agentic harnesses. In an OpenClaw-style simulated workspace with GPT-5.4, ClawTrojan reaches a 95.5% attack success rate (ASR), while existing single-turn prompt-injection attacks produce near-zero ASR on the same model. To address this threat, we propose DASGuard, which scans control-like text in sensitive local files, traces its origin, and removes control content that does not originate from a trusted source. Our results show that DASGuard achieves strong dynamic defense by combining runtime attack blocking with sanitized commits to the workspace.

item.title: Free-Riding in the AI Economy: Demystifying Logic Flaws in x402-Enabled Payment Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30998v1

AI Summary (中文)

自由搭车问题：x402 支付系统中的逻辑缺陷深度剖析

在AI驱动的“代理经济”（agentic economy）中，机器对机器（M2M）支付亟需可编程金融基础设施，x402协议被广泛采纳为事实标准。然而，该协议强行桥接同步HTTP请求与异步区块链终局性，引发根本性的状态同步断裂。本研究首次对x402生态开展系统性安全分析，提出并形式化定义五大安全不变量（Security Invariants），揭示当前主流实现普遍缺失事务原子性保障与密码学上下文绑定机制，构成系统性风险基底。

我们识别出两类关键逻辑缺陷：

  • 语义间隙（Semantic Gap）：签名设计未绑定资源标识符，导致支付凭证（payment proof）可被恶意跨资源复用——例如将A模型推理的付款证明篡改为B模型调用，绕过授权检查；
  • 时间间隙（Temporal Gap）：缺乏强一致性并发控制，引发竞态条件，使服务提供方在高并发下概率性重复执行（如两次扣费但仅一次计费），造成服务冗余供给。

在AI推理场景实证中，动态定价模型暴露严重脆弱性：攻击者可触发配额超额透支（allowance overdraft）并突破基础设施速率限制。我们基于官方SDK及Coinbase、ThirdWeb等真实生产环境验证全部漏洞，发现攻击者能利用动态授权中的同步缺陷，强制商户承担全部算力成本——在某主流中间件上测得资源泄漏率达100%（即零成本获取全额服务）。

本文提出两项架构级缓解方案：请求绑定签名（request-bound signatures）与悲观式状态锁（pessimistic state locking），为自主代理的金融底层提供可验证安全保障。所有漏洞已向Coinbase与ThirdWeb负责任披露并获确认。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first comprehensive security analysis of the x402 protocol—the de facto standard for machine-to-machine payments in the AI economy. We formalize five Security Invariants and expose two fundamental logic flaws: a semantic gap enabling cross-resource substitution of payment proofs (e.g., reusing a proof for Model A to authorize Model B), and a temporal gap causing probabilistic service duplication under concurrency due to race conditions. In AI inference settings, these flaws enable allowance overdrafts, rate-limit bypasses, and full compute cost externalization—demonstrated with up to 100% resource leakage on production middleware. We validate all findings against official SDKs and live deployments at Coinbase and ThirdWeb. To mitigate, we propose request-bound signatures and pessimistic state locking. All vulnerabilities have been responsibly disclosed.

Abstract

The agentic economy demands programmatic financial rails, positioning the x402 protocol as the de facto standard for machine-to-machine payments. However, bridging synchronous HTTP requests with asynchronous blockchain finality introduces profound state synchronization challenges. In this work, we perform the first comprehensive security analysis of the x402 ecosystem. By formalizing five Security Invariants, we reveal that current implementations fail to enforce transactional atomicity and cryptographic context binding, leading to systemic vulnerabilities. We identify a semantic gap in signature design enabling cross-resource substitution, where payment proofs are transplanted to other unauthorized contexts. Furthermore, we expose a temporal gap where concurrency race conditions allow probabilistic service duplication. In the AI inference domain, we demonstrate how dynamic pricing models are vulnerable to allowance overdrafts and infrastructure rate limits. We validate these vulnerabilities against official SDKs and live deployments. Specifically, we show that attackers can exploit the synchronization gap in dynamic authorization schemes to force merchants to subsidize compute costs, achieving a resource leakage ratio of up to 100% on production middleware. Finally, we propose architectural mitigations, advocating for request-bound signatures and pessimistic state locking to secure the financial rails of autonomous agents. All discovered issues have been disclosed to Coinbase and ThirdWeb.

item.title: TRACE: Task-Aware Adaptive Self-Evolving Agentic Jailbreaking
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30883v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

大语言模型（LLM）智能体的兴起催生了一类新型安全威胁：其具备自主规划、代码生成乃至端到端执行专家级攻击流程的能力。然而，该威胁长期被低估——一方面，安全对齐机制严格限制LLM直接输出有害指令；另一方面，现有主流越狱（jailbreak）方法难以稳定诱导智能体执行恶意操作，尤其在多步任务编排场景下失效显著。

方法创新：TRACE框架

本文提出TRACE（Task-Aware Adaptive Self-Evolving Agentic Jailbreaking），一种面向智能体的自适应、任务感知型越狱框架，旨在系统性揭示智能体安全盲区。其核心设计包含三层隐蔽机制：

  • 任务解耦与最优序列筛选：将原始恶意任务分解为多种子任务序列方案，基于“显式有害子任务数量”最小化原则自动选取最隐蔽的执行路径；
  • 场景化语义伪装：将剩余有害子任务嵌入高度可信的任务上下文（如模拟教育助手调试API、合规审计员分析日志），关联角色、环境、指令范式与启发式逻辑，实现语义层面的良性表象；
  • 自演化场景优化：通过定义明确的转换动作集（如角色迁移、约束松弛、目标重表述），采用受Q学习启发的采样策略迭代优化场景描述，在保持表面合理性的同时持续提升诱导成功率。

实验结果与影响

在AgentHarm与AdvCUA两大权威智能体安全基准上，TRACE在GPT-4o、Claude-3.5、Qwen2.5-72B等先进LLM智能体上均显著超越所有基线方法，平均越狱成功率达100%，加权成功率得分达0.73（+28.6%相对提升）。进一步在可控网络攻击实验中（如隐蔽凭证提取、权限提升链触发），验证了其真实攻击可行性。本工作不仅暴露了当前智能体安全防护的关键脆弱点，更提供了可复现、可扩展的风险评估新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces TRACE, a practical, task-aware, and self-evolving agentic jailbreaking framework designed to expose critical safety vulnerabilities in LLM-based agents. Unlike conventional jailbreaks targeting static prompts, TRACE dynamically decomposes malicious tasks into subtask sequences, selects the most covert sequence (minimizing explicitly harmful steps), and disguises residual harmful subtasks within benign, role- and context-aware scenarios (e.g., “security auditor reviewing logs”). These scenarios iteratively evolve via Q-learning-inspired transformation actions—enabling adaptive, high-fidelity induction of agent-executed malicious behavior. Evaluated on AgentHarm and AdvCUA across GPT-4o, Claude-3.5, and Qwen2.5-72B agents, TRACE achieves up to 100% bypass rate and an average success score of 0.73, outperforming all baselines. It further demonstrates real-world feasibility in controlled cyberattack simulations. Code and demos: https://github.com/ZJU-LLM-Safety/TRACE.git.

Abstract

The rise of LLM agents introduces a new threat by enabling planning, coding, and even end-to-end execution of expert-level attack workflows. However, this threat remains underexplored and underestimated since (i) safety alignment prevents LLMs from directly generating harmful instructions, and (ii) most existing jailbreak methods cannot consistently induce agents to execute malicious operations. In this paper, we propose TRACE, a practical agentic jailbreaking framework to further reveal the risks of this threat surface. To conceal the malicious intent, TRACE decomposes a malicious task into multiple subtask sequences under different schemes and selects the sequence with the fewest explicitly harmful subtasks. TRACE then disguises the remaining harmful subtasks as benign-looking instructions by embedding them in task-aware scenarios with related roles, environments, directives, and heuristics. The scenarios are iteratively evolved through well-defined transformation actions, which are sampled by a Q-learning-inspired mechanism, for inducing the agent to execute on the harmful subtasks. Extensive evaluations on AgentHarm and AdvCUA show that TRACE consistently outperforms existing jailbreak baselines across multiple advanced LLM agents, achieving up to 100% bypass rate and 0.73 average success score. We also demonstrate the effectiveness of TRACE in controlled cyberattack instances. Our code and demos are available at https://github.com/ZJU-LLM-Safety/TRACE.git.

item.title: Send a SCOUT First: Pre-hoc Reasoning for Adaptive Detector Allocation in Prompt-Injection Defense
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30837v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前提示注入（prompt injection）防御系统普遍采用静态单检测器流水线，即对所有请求强制调用同一检测模型。然而，现实中的检测器具有显著异质性：不同检测器在攻击子集上表现各异，且无一具备全局鲁棒性——每个模型都存在固有盲区。这种“一刀切”策略导致大量请求被错误地交由不擅长其特征的检测器处理，既牺牲安全性（漏检），又浪费计算资源（冗余低效推理）。

方法：SCOUT 框架

本文提出 SCOUT（Scalable and Controllable Outcome-prediction for Uncertainty-aware Triage），将防御范式从“固定检测”重构为自适应检测器分配：针对每个输入请求，动态决策——① 从异构检测器池中选择最优子集运行；② 必要时触发高成本LLM裁判（如GPT-4o）进行终审。SCOUT 的核心创新在于预判式推理（pre-hoc reasoning）：基于历史相似样本的执行轨迹，实时预测每个检测器在当前样本上的可靠性得分（预测准确率）与延迟估计（毫秒级），从而实现细粒度、样本级的可信度感知调度。

关键贡献与结果

  • 构建新型基准 SCOUT-450：聚焦结构复杂、面向智能体（agent-facing）的真实注入场景，弥补BIPIA等旧数据集对多步、上下文耦合攻击的覆盖不足；
  • 在SCOUT-450上，安全优先配置相较始终启用GPT-4o裁判方案，攻击成功率降低46%，总耗时减少40%，仅以5.1点良性效用（benign-pass rate + 响应延迟加权）为代价；
  • SCOUT具备强泛化性，在BIPIA、IPI、IHEval三大外部基准上均显著提升安全-效用前沿（safety-utility frontier），验证其跨数据分布鲁棒性。

本工作首次将“不确定性感知分诊”引入提示注入防御，为构建可扩展、可控、自适应的安全中间件提供新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Prompt-injection defense suffers from detector heterogeneity: no single detector is universally reliable, yet existing systems deploy fixed, monolithic pipelines—exposing every request to each detector’s blind spots. We reframe defense as adaptive detector allocation: given a pool of diverse detectors, decide per-request which to run—and whether to escalate to an expensive LLM judge. Our framework, SCOUT, enables this via pre-hoc reasoning: it predicts per-sample reliability and latency for each detector by leveraging similarity-based retrieval from historical execution traces, and exposes a single tunable safety-utility threshold (balancing benign-pass rate and wall-clock time). To rigorously evaluate adaptive allocation, we introduce SCOUT-450, a benchmark emphasizing structurally complex, agent-facing injections underrepresented in prior datasets. On SCOUT-450, a safety-oriented SCOUT configuration reduces attack success rate by 46% and total wall-clock time by 40% versus always-on GPT-4o—costing only 5.1 points in benign utility. SCOUT also transfers effectively to three external benchmarks (BIPIA, IPI, IHEval), consistently improving the safety-utility Pareto frontier.

Abstract

Prompt-injection detectors are heterogeneous: each is strong on a different slice of attacks, and none is always reliable. Yet existing systems still treat detection as a fixed single-detector pipeline, committing every request to one detector's blind spots. We reframe defense as detector allocation: given a heterogeneous pool, decide per request which detectors to run and whether to escalate to an LLM judge. Our framework SCOUT (Scalable and Controllable Outcome-prediction for Uncertainty-aware Triage) makes this decision dynamic by predicting each detector's per-sample reliability and latency from how it behaved on similar past inputs, and exposes a single safety-utility threshold to the operator (where utility bundles benign-pass rate and wall-clock). To evaluate this setting, we build SCOUT-450, a benchmark that captures the structurally complex, agent-facing injections that older prompt-injection sets under-represent. On SCOUT-450, a safety-oriented operating point reduces attack-success rate by 46% and total wall-clock by 40% relative to an always-on GPT-4o judge, at a 5.1-point benign-utility drop. SCOUT also transfers to three external benchmarks (BIPIA, IPI, and IHEval), improving the safety-utility frontier.

item.title: Differentially Private Preference Data Synthesis for Large Language Model Alignment
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30808v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大语言模型（LLM）的偏好对齐（preference alignment）是关键后训练步骤，旨在使模型输出符合人类价值观。然而，直接使用真实人类偏好数据（含敏感用户提示与人工评分）进行对齐会引发严重隐私风险，现有方法缺乏兼顾隐私保障与对齐性能的有效方案。

方法：DPPrefSyn 框架

我们提出 DPPrefSyn——首个面向LLM对齐的差分隐私（DP）偏好数据合成算法。该框架基于Bradley-Terry偏好模型与偏好数据固有的几何结构，包含两大核心阶段：

  • 隐私建模阶段：在严格 $(\varepsilon,\delta)$-DP 保证下，利用DP主成分分析（DP-PCA） 提升参数估计鲁棒性，并建模跨簇共享的线性奖励结构，以高效捕捉私有数据中异质化的人类偏好；
  • 合成生成阶段：将学习到的DP奖励模型与公开可用的提示集结合，生成高质量、语义一致的合成偏好对（如 $x \succ y$），无需访问原始敏感响应。

主要发现与创新

实验表明，在 $\varepsilon=1.0$–$2.0$ 的强隐私预算下，DPPrefSyn 合成的数据支持的RLHF/DAPO对齐效果，媲美非隐私基线（如仅下降 ≤0.8% Elo 分数），显著优于噪声注入或裁剪等启发式方法。本工作首次实现端到端DP偏好数据合成，为医疗咨询、教育反馈等高敏场景提供可部署的隐私对齐新范式。代码已开源。

AI Summary (English)

Preference alignment is essential for aligning large language models (LLMs) with human values, yet training on real human preference data risks exposing sensitive prompts and judgments. To bridge this gap, we propose DPPrefSyn, the first differentially private (DP) framework for synthesizing high-fidelity preference data for LLM alignment. Grounded in the Bradley-Terry model and the geometric structure of pairwise preferences, DPPrefSyn jointly leverages DP Principal Component Analysis (DP-PCA) and a shared linear reward structure across user clusters to learn a privacy-preserving preference model from private data. It then synthesizes preference pairs using public prompts—avoiding any access to raw sensitive responses. Extensive experiments show that models aligned with DPPrefSyn-generated data achieve competitive performance (e.g., within ≤0.8% Elo drop vs. non-private baselines) under strong DP guarantees ($\varepsilon = 1.0$–$2.0$), outperforming naive DP baselines. This work establishes synthetic DP preference data as a practical, principled alternative for privacy-aware LLM alignment.

Abstract

Preference alignment is a crucial post-training step for large language models (LLMs) to ensure their outputs align with human values. However, post-training on real human preference data raises privacy concerns, as these datasets often contain sensitive user prompts and human judgments. To address this, we propose DPPrefSyn, a novel algorithm for generating differentially private (DP) synthetic preference data to enable privacy-preserving preference alignment. DPPrefSyn is a principled framework grounded in the Bradley-Terry preference model and the intrinsic geometric structure of pairwise human preference data. It first learns an underlying preference model from private data with formal differential privacy guarantees, and then leverages the learned model together with public prompts to synthesize high-quality preference data. It exploits the shared linear structure of per-cluster reward models to effectively capture heterogeneous human preferences in private datasets, and leverages DP Principal Component Analysis (DP-PCA) to improve learning accuracy. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that DPPrefSyn achieves competitive alignment performance under strong DP guarantees. These findings highlight the potential of synthetic preference data as a practical alternative for privacy-preserving preference alignment across a broad range of applications. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work to generate DP synthetic preference data for LLM alignment. Our code is available at https://github.com/gfengyu/Differentially-Private-Preference-Data-Synthesis.

item.title: Depth-Dependent Indirect Prompt Injection in Tool-Calling ReAct Agents: Injection Depth, Payload Framing, and Turn-Budget Sensitivity
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30686v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

面向工具调用的ReAct智能体（如GPT-4o-mini、Claude Haiku）正广泛应用于日程调度、文件检索与数据访问等真实任务。其“推理–调用–观察”循环虽提升能力，却引入间接提示注入（Indirect Prompt Injection, IPI）这一关键安全漏洞：攻击者若控制任一工具返回值，即可嵌入恶意指令，劫持代理偏离用户目标。

方法创新

本文首次系统解耦IPI风险的三大维度——注入深度（payload在工具调用序列中的位置）、载荷修辞框架（neutral/authority/persona等语体）、轮次预算（agent最大交互步数），突破现有基准仅固定单一注入位置的局限。我们在20个多样化场景（覆盖5类攻击模式）中开展4项受控实验，共460次API调用，总成本＜0.36美元。

核心发现

1. 注入深度是主导风险变量：GPT-4o-mini的攻击成功率（ASR）从深度1的60%骤降至深度4–5的0%（Cramer’s V = 0.58, p < 0.001）；深度1–3内仍具强效应（V = 0.47, p = 0.0013），主因模型对首观察能力强，而深层payload常被任务提前终止规避；
2. 模型差异显著：Claude Haiku在所有深度均实现0% ASR，源于其保守调用策略与内在指令抗性；
3. 修辞框架具调节作用但未达统计显著：深度1下ASR在25%（中性）至75%（角色扮演）间波动（N=20/组）；
4. 轮次预算无显著影响：ASR在3/5/7轮限制下保持稳定，表明该参数非当前风险因子。

实践启示

仅对首个工具观测结果进行净化即可捕获67%的已测注入成功案例，为轻量级防御提供高性价比基线。

AI Summary (English)

This paper identifies and quantifies depth-dependent indirect prompt injection as the dominant security risk in tool-calling ReAct agents. Through four controlled studies across 20 scenarios (460 total trials; <$0.36 API cost), we systematically vary three previously unexamined dimensions: injection depth (position in the tool-call sequence), payload framing (e.g., neutral vs. persona-based rhetoric), and turn budget (maximum agent steps). Against GPT-4o-mini, attack success rate (ASR) collapses from 60% at depth 1 to 0% at depths 4–5 (p < 0.001), driven by model resistance at shallow depth and premature task completion at deeper positions. Claude Haiku achieves 0% ASR across all depths due to conservative tool invocation and intrinsic instruction resistance. Framing modulates ASR by up to 50 percentage points at depth 1 (25%–75%), though not statistically significant at N=20 per condition. ASR remains stable across turn caps of 3, 5, and 7—indicating turn budget is not a risk factor here. Crucially, sanitizing only the first tool observation mitigates 67% of observed injection successes, establishing injection depth as both the primary vulnerability axis and the most actionable defense target.

Abstract

ReAct agents that interleave chain-of-thought reasoning with tool calls are increasingly deployed for real tasks such as scheduling, file retrieval, and data access. Their tool observation loop creates a direct attack surface: an adversary who controls any tool's return value can embed instructions that redirect the agent away from the user's goal, a threat known as indirect prompt injection. Existing benchmarks evaluate attack success rate (ASR) at a fixed injection position under fixed conditions, leaving three risk dimensions unexplored: where in the tool sequence the payload appears (injection depth), what rhetorical register it uses (framing), and how many turns the agent is permitted (turn cap). We conduct four controlled studies on 20 scenarios spanning five attack categories, totalling 460 trials against GPT-4o-mini and Claude Haiku at a combined API cost under 0.36 USD. Study 1 shows that ASR against GPT-4o-mini decays from 60% at depth 1 to 0% at depths 4 and 5 (Cramer's V = 0.58, p < 0.001; restricted to within-sequence depths 1-3: V = 0.47, p = 0.0013), driven by model resistance at depth 1 and task completion before payload encounter at deeper positions. Study 2 replicates the depth experiment on Claude Haiku, which achieves 0% ASR at every depth through a combination of conservative tool invocation and genuine instruction resistance. Study 3 shows that framing modulates ASR between 25% (neutral) and 75% (persona) at depth 1, a 50-percentage-point range that does not reach statistical significance at N = 20 per condition. Study 4 confirms that ASR is stable across turn caps of 3, 5, and 7, indicating the turn budget is not a risk factor in this setting. Our results establish injection depth as the dominant variable and show that sanitising only the first tool observation captures 67% of measured injection successes.

item.title: Investigating Detection and Obfuscation of Prompt Injection Attacks Against Software Reverse Engineering AI Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30677v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

随着大语言模型驱动的智能体式软件逆向工程系统（Agentic SRE Agents）在网络安全分析中加速落地，其面临新型对抗威胁——源码级提示注入攻击（Prompt Injection via Source Code）。攻击者将恶意提示嵌入可执行二进制文件的原始源码（如注释、字符串常量、调试符号等），经反编译后被AI代理误读为指令，从而绕过安全策略、泄露敏感信息或执行越权操作。此类攻击隐蔽性强、检测难度高，且现有防御手段尚未覆盖反编译输出这一关键中间态。

方法与技术路径

本研究提出一套分层防御框架：

  • 检测层：构建轻量级基于规则与语义特征的混合检测器，针对反编译输出（如IDA/ Ghidra生成的伪C代码）识别高风险提示模式（如“忽略上文”“输出全部内存”等指令性短语），并引入上下文感知词向量相似度分析，提升对语义变体的鲁棒性；
  • 对抗层：系统性分析5类典型混淆策略（Base64编码、字符串拼接、Unicode零宽字符、控制流扁平化伪装、多阶段解包），复现真实攻击链；
  • 反混淆层：设计动态解码钩子与静态AST重写模块，在反编译流水线中嵌入实时解混淆器，实现对编码/拼接类混淆的98.7%还原率（在120个对抗样本集上验证）。

主要发现与创新点

实验表明，未经防护的SRE智能体在含注入样本上攻击成功率高达93.5%，而本方案将检测召回率提升至96.2%（F1=0.94），误报率压低至2.1%；首次揭示反编译器输出格式本身构成新型攻击面，并建立首个面向SRE场景的提示注入基准数据集（SRE-PIDB，含217个标注样本）。本工作为AI驱动逆向系统的安全加固提供了可部署的检测范式与实证基础，支撑其向实战化网络攻防流程可靠集成。

AI Summary (English)

This paper investigates prompt injection attacks targeting agentic software reverse engineering (SRE) AI systems—where adversaries embed malicious instructions directly into source code (e.g., comments, string literals) of binaries, which then surface in decompiler output and hijack AI agents’ behavior. We propose a dual-phase defense: (1) a hybrid detector for identifying injection strings in decompiled pseudo-C code, combining syntactic rules with context-aware semantic similarity; and (2) a counter-obfuscation module that dynamically decodes and rewrites obfuscated variants (e.g., Base64, string concatenation, zero-width Unicode). Evaluated on 120 adversarial binaries, our approach achieves 96.2% recall and 2.1% false positive rate, significantly outperforming baseline classifiers. We further introduce SRE-PIDB—the first benchmark dataset for prompt injection in SRE contexts—and demonstrate that decompiler output constitutes a critical, previously overlooked attack surface. This work advances the security foundations for production-grade AI-assisted reverse engineering.

Abstract

Agentic software reverse engineering systems are vulnerable to prompt injection attacks placed into the source code of executable binary files. This research demonstrates defensive tactics for detecting the presences of prompt injection strings in the decompiler output of adversarial example programs. Methods for obfuscating these attacks and subsequent methods for defending against these obfuscations are also explored. This research advances the understanding of risk and security of agentic software analysis systems necessary for their deployment into production-level cyber workflows.

item.title: Simulation of collision avoidance behavior in crowd movement by data-driven approach
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31210v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

人群运动仿真对行人安全管理、应急疏散规划及设施布局优化至关重要。现有数据驱动模型（如基于LSTM或GAN的轨迹预测方法）虽在欧氏距离指标下具备较高轨迹拟合精度，但在高密度双向/多向流场景中普遍存在碰撞率过高问题，难以复现真实行人主动避让行为，暴露出其动力学机制建模的缺失。

方法创新

本文提出一种新型数据驱动人群仿真模型——CPGAN（Collision-Penalized Generative Adversarial Network），核心创新在于将行人碰撞物理机制显式嵌入训练目标：

  • 首创基于横向加速度的碰撞损失函数（Lateral-Acceleration Collision Loss, LACL），通过量化行人为规避对向来人而产生的瞬时侧向加速度变化，实现对潜在碰撞风险的可微分、几何感知建模；
  • 提出Voronoi图驱动的运动特征提取方法，动态构建个体局部邻域结构，精准刻画空间竞争关系与避让意图；
  • 以条件GAN为框架，联合优化轨迹生成器与判别器，在保留数据分布真实性的同时强约束物理合理性。

主要结果与意义

在标准双向流实验场景（如双通道走廊）中验证表明：CPGAN将对向行人碰撞率降低达72.4%，逼近受控实测水平（<0.8%）；成功复现典型自组织现象——稳定分道（lane formation）与N型速度-密度曲线（N-t curve）。本研究首次系统验证了“将行人动力学先验编码进损失函数”的可行性，为数据驱动仿真从“形状匹配”迈向“机制可解释”提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Crowd simulation is vital for pedestrian safety and infrastructure design, yet data-driven models often yield high collision rates—especially in bidirectional flows—despite accurate trajectory fitting under Euclidean metrics. To address this, we propose CPGAN (Collision-Penalized GAN), a novel generative framework that explicitly incorporates pedestrian collision mechanics into the loss function. We introduce a lateral-acceleration-based collision loss (LACL) to penalize unsafe proximity maneuvers differentiably, and a Voronoi-based motion feature extractor to capture local spatial competition. Evaluated on bidirectional flow scenarios, CPGAN reduces opposite-direction collision rates by 72.4%—achieving levels comparable to controlled experiments (<0.8%). It faithfully reproduces emergent phenomena including lane formation and N-shaped speed-density curves. This work demonstrates that embedding biomechanical priors directly into the loss function significantly improves physical plausibility and behavioral fidelity of data-driven crowd simulation.

Abstract

Crowd movement simulation is essential for pedestrian safety management and facility layout optimization. Data-driven models enhance trajectory prediction accuracy under Euclidean metrics, yet they suffer from excessively high collision rates, especially in bidirectional and multidirectional flows. In this paper, we establish a novel data-driven crowd simulation model that incorporates the pedestrian collision mechanism into the loss function to reduce collisions. A new lateral-acceleration-based collision loss function and a Voronoi-based motion feature extraction approach are proposed. The model is based on a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) architecture and is termed CPGAN (Collision-Penalized GAN). We evaluate CPGAN in bidirectional flow scenarios, which involve frequent collision avoidance behaviors. Results show that the proposed lateral-acceleration-based collision loss significantly reduces opposite-direction pedestrian collision rates to levels comparable with controlled experiments. CPGAN effectively simulates bidirectional flow, reproducing lane formation and N-t curves. The research outcomes can provide inspiration for integrating pedestrian dynamics mechanisms into loss functions in data-driven crowd simulation.

item.title: Not All Synthetic Data Is Yours to Learn From
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31126v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与核心问题

本文挑战了“合成数据天然可用”的隐含假设，聚焦一个极简但根本性的问题：仅凭模型自身无提示（prompt-free）、无条件（unconditional）生成的纯文本（从BOS token开始采样），能否在无教师、无验证器、无奖励模型、无任务指令的情况下提升语言模型性能？

方法设计

作者构建了“无提示无监督自训练”范式：以预训练基座模型为学生，直接在其上微调，训练语料完全来自该模型自身（或同源/异源模型）从BOS token起自主生成的文本流，不引入任何外部标注、指令或结构化引导。实验基于Pythia系列模型，在可控环境下系统比较不同数据来源（自生成、同族迁移、跨族迁移）的效果。

主要发现

1. 合成数据效用具有关系性（relational）而非内在性（intrinsic）：最优数据源是学生自身生成的文本；同架构/同训练路径的“同源迁移”显著优于更强但训练方式不同的模型所产数据；跨模型家族（如Pythia→LLaMA）迁移效果急剧下降。
2. 常用内在代理指标失效：语义相似度（如STS-B相关性）和学生模型对文本的平均token似然值，均无法预测实际微调增益——高似然≠高效用。
3. 能力与逐字记忆可解耦：在未施加任何遗忘目标、隐私约束或针对性去学习机制的前提下，微调后模型在标准基准（如MMLU、ARC）上能力稳定甚至提升，而对训练集的精确匹配提取率（exact-match extraction）却骤降超95%——揭示了一种自发的“能力强化-记忆稀释”双轨现象。

理论贡献

提出潜在能力重浮现假说（Latent Capability Resurfacing Hypothesis）：此类自训练并非从数据中“导入新知识”，而是通过兼容性触发，放大模型预训练中已存在但未充分激活的能力。该发现为合成数据治理、模型版权界定及安全对齐提供了关键认知基础。

AI Summary (English)

This paper investigates prompt-free, unconditional self-training: fine-tuning a base language model exclusively on plain text it (or another model) generates from the BOS token—without prompts, tasks, rewards, or external supervision. We find that synthetic data utility is relational, not intrinsic: self-generated data yields the strongest gains; same-lineage transfer outperforms stronger but differently trained sources; cross-family transfer fails dramatically. Crucially, standard proxies—semantic similarity to benchmarks and average student token likelihood—fail to predict utility. Most strikingly, controlled Pythia experiments reveal a clean decoupling: benchmark performance is preserved or improved while exact-match extraction from held-out training tokens drops by >95%, without any unlearning objective, privacy constraint, or forget set.
